rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEILIN WANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator requests additional fees for extraordinary services in the total amount of $5,050. The statutory fees payable to the administrator for ordinary services will be $24,264.28. The administrator did not maintain supporting time records for her ordinary services. 2) The administrator's attorneys request additional fees for extraordinary services in the total amount of $33,341. The attorneys' services claimed to be extraordinary include those related to the petitioner's appointment as administrator ($924.50) and preparation of this final accounting ($1,575). rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA WILLIAMS-SWINT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Reduce Bond
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court's 12/20/2023 appointment order fixed the petitioner/administrator's bond at $1,201,000 equal to estimated net value of the estate real property alleged in the petition for probate, even though only limited IAEA authority was granted. The petition for probate alleged estimated value of personal property (only bondable assets in case of limited IAEA authority) was $0. Neither the minutes of the 12/20/2023 hearing nor supplemental papers filed before the hearing identified specific value of any personal property or income in support of the $1,201,000 bond. Probate Code section 8482(a) provides that "the court in its discretion may fix the amount of the bond, but the amount of the bond shall be not more than the sum of: (1) The estimated value of the personal property; (2) The probable annual gross income of the estate; and (3) If independent administration is granted as to real property, the estimated value of the decedent's interest in the real property. The Court is to determine whether to correct the appointment order nunc pro tunc on the Court's own motion under CCP section 473(d) to fix the initial bond amount at $0, in which event an amended order for probate (DE-140 form) must be submitted before the clerk can issue letters and before this petition can be adjudicated to require a $450,000 bond as prayed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MONSERRATE MAGOBET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proposed executor is the administrator of the predeceased son's estate, against whom this decedent filed a claim in case CGC-21-594479. He cannot be the personal representative of the son's estate defending against himself as the personal representative for this decedent's estate. It appears that a third party should be appointed as the administrator with will annexed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN WILLIAM BARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/23/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Lack of courtesy copies can delay review of a matter. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No courtesy copies or a proposed order were received by the assigned examiner. Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) that stands on its own, not in response to issues posted in a tentative ruling or to "Examiner's Notes." There is also no need to narrate that a correct form of notice will be mailed; it should just be mailed and a courtesy copy provided.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PAMELA LYNN CLARK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Petition to set forth the petitioner's standing as he is a beneficially interested person. (2) It is not clear why the fact of death needs to be judicially established when the person allegedly died in a hospital. Petition merely states there is no record, and petitioner was unable to find a record. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
